common.available: EU citizen · Aarhus, Denmark · Open to new opportunities, on-site or remote across CET ±2

home.heroSubtitle: I'm Eduard, a Software Engineer at Mjølner Informatics in Aarhus. Calm under complexity, meticulous about quality, and motivated by long-term solutions for business-critical systems.
home.aboutP1: I'm a Software Engineer and IT Consultant based in Aarhus, with five-plus years across the public sector, defence, retail and renewable-energy SaaS. The throughline is quality-focused delivery: backend and frontend feature work, requirements clarification, automated testing, and the kind of stakeholder coordination that lowers delivery risk. I joined Mjølner Informatics in April 2026 after closing my chapter at Netcompany; before that came Greenbyte, Boozt, and Systematic.
home.aboutP2: Romanian by birth, Danish by residence, European by inclination. I hold an MSc in Engineering — Technology-Based Business Development from Aarhus University (2022 – 2024), and a BSc in Software Technology Engineering from VIA University College in Horsens. The marketing diploma I started with at IBA Kolding wasn't wasted — it left me with a habit of reading a brief carefully before writing a line of code.
home.aboutP3Lead: What I look for in a team: meaningful systems, calm review culture, and colleagues who treat correctness as the long game.
home.aboutP3LinksHint: The /my-story page has the longer arc; the /now page has whatever has my attention this month.

writing.articlesLead: Academic writing from my master's programme at Aarhus University.

recommends.heading: Things I've used and would buy again.
recommends.description: A small, honest collection of products, tools, and books I keep coming back to, with the reasoning, not just the link.

personal.heading: What I do when I'm not at the keyboard.
personal.description: BVB on Saturdays, the open road on weekends, and the slow accumulation of countries on a wall map. The professional half is the easy half: this one's a bit more honest.

sections.footballLede: Borussia Dortmund: the most beautiful chaos in football. Tuesday training, Saturday kickoff, Sunday post-mortems.
sections.footballBody: I picked up Dortmund years ago and never let go — Westfalenstadion when the schedule and the budget align, the rest from the sofa, in the rhythm of the Bundesliga calendar. It is a steady kind of fandom: not built on trophies, kept up because the team is mine and because Saturdays are better with a match on.
sections.carsLede: Driving as decompression. Long detours through Jutland, the occasional autobahn run, an embarrassing amount of music.
sections.travelHeading: A wall of countries, slowly filling
sections.travelLede: Slowly accumulating stamps and memories. Cities for the food, mountains for the silence, coastlines for the wind.

travel.heading: Notes from the road.
travel.description: Trips, places, pictures, slowly accumulating. The non-engineering side of the brain.

contact.heading: Let's talk.
contact.description: Recruiters, fellow engineers, fellow consultants: drop a line. I read everything and reply within a few days.
